narration.text: Welcome to the Clawrium Quickstart — a walkthrough of installing and chatting with your first agent.

Here's the full walkthrough: check our clawctl version, initialize the service, register a host, install a hermes agent, configure it with a provider, verify the fleet is up, then chat with our new agent.
This demo runs on clawctl 26.6.4 — everything you see was recorded against this release.
First, initialize the clawrium service. This creates the config directory and validates that Ansible and SSH are available.
Next, register the host you'll deploy agents to. We're pointing at wolf-i, a machine already on the network.
Now install a hermes agent on wolf-i. Clawrium uses Ansible under the hood — this is a real install playbook running against the remote host.
Skipping ahead — Ansible's TASK lines configure dependencies, lay down the systemd unit, and verify port allocations.
Configure the agent. We're attaching a litellm-backed provider from the secret store so it can actually answer prompts.
Check fleet status. The new quickstart-demo agent is up and running.
Finally, chat with it. One prompt in, one response out — clawrium just routed the request through the configured provider.
